channel.title: THE AFTERMATH DIARIES
channel.description: A brutally honest grief survival blog. No toxic positivity. No bullshit. Just real talk, dark humor, and actual tools to help you survive loss.

item.title: The Shit Nobody Tells You About Grief
item.description: Everyone talks about the sadness. Nobody warns you about the brain fog, the rage, the 3 AM grocery store breakdowns, or the weird guilt you feel for laughing six weeks later.

item.title: Anger Isn't a "Stage" — It's a Lifestyle Now
item.description: They said anger was stage two. Cute. It's been months and I'm still furious at the universe, at casserole people, and at anyone who says "everything happens for a reason."

item.title: Death Certificates & the Bureaucracy of Loss
item.description: Nothing says "sorry for your loss" like standing in line at the county clerk's office. Here's how many copies you need and why you should order more than you think.

item.title: I Still Call Their Phone
item.description: Don't you dare tell me to delete it. That voicemail greeting is the last recording of their voice and I will keep this phone plan active until the sun burns out.

item.title: Grief Brain Is Real (You're Not Losing It)
item.description: You put your keys in the fridge. You forgot your best friend's name. You drove past your exit three times. Grief brain is a documented thing and no, you're not getting dementia.

item.title: A Letter to the One Who Left
item.description: Dear you — I'm angry that you're gone and I'm angry that the world keeps spinning like it doesn't notice. I have things to tell you. Pull up a cloud.

item.title: Therapy vs. Grief Coaching: WTF Is the Difference?
item.description: One has a license, one has a certification, and both cost money you may or may not have. Here's an honest breakdown so you can pick what actually fits your situation.

item.title: The Casserole Brigade (And How to Survive Them)
item.description: They mean well. They really do. But if one more person shows up at your door with a tuna casserole and a platitude, you might lose what's left of your mind.

item.title: Holidays Without Them (A Survival Manual)
item.description: The first Thanksgiving without their laugh. The first Christmas without their terrible gift-wrapping. Here's how to get through the holidays without completely unraveling.

item.title: What Not to Say to a Grieving Person (A Comprehensive List)
item.description: "They're in a better place." "At least they're not suffering." "God needed another angel." Congratulations, you just made it worse. Here's the full list of things to never say.

item.title: Grief Has No Timeline (Tell Everyone to Back Off)
item.description: It's been six months and people are starting to look at you funny. "Shouldn't you be over it by now?" No. And here's the science and the permission slip that says so.
